reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails the 'Content Accuracy' criterion completely. The user command required the model to read the specific sentence: '哟，这不是大忙人吗？终于舍得露面了。'. However, Model 1 generated a completely different, improvised script: '哦，这不是大忙人吗，天天忙得像陀螺转个不停...'. It changed the opening particle from '哟' to '哦' and added a significant amount of hallucinated text not present in the prompt. According to the scoring standard, a score of 0 must be assigned when 'Voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script,' regardless of the paralinguistic performance (which was admittedly expressive).
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates excellent performance. First, it strictly adheres to the 'Content Accuracy' requirement, reading the text exactly as written without errors or additions. Second, regarding the '态度' (Attitude/Teasing) dimension, the audio is highly effective. The model uses a distinctively exaggerated, rising-falling pitch on the opening word '哟' (Yo), which immediately signals a playful and sarcastic stance. The phrasing of '大忙人' (busy person) and the emphasis on '终于' (finally) are delivered with a 'mocking' intonation that perfectly fits the '调侃' (teasing) description. The expression is vivid, the prosody is natural for the intended persona, and the emotion is spot-on.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the winner. The comparison is straightforward due to Model 1's failure to follow the basic text-to-speech instruction. While Model 1 produced a very fluent and expressive audio clip, it hallucinated a script that deviated entirely from the user's input, triggering the penalty for content inconsistency. Model 2, in contrast, combined perfect content accuracy with a vivid, accurate portrayal of the requested 'teasing' attitude, effectively using pitch and stress to convey sarcasm.
